prose:
Fukui-reactivity descriptors for nitrobenzene were computed using DFT with B3LYP functional and def2-TZVP basis set (tier 'standard', PySCF backend) in the gas phase at charge 0, multiplicity 1. The calculation evaluated the electrophilicity (f⁺), nucleophilicity (f⁻), and dual descriptor for each atom by computing Mulliken partial charges in the neutral ground state (N), the N+1 cation (multiplicity 2), and the N−1 anion (multiplicity 2). Both perturbed states converged successfully. The most electrophilic site is the ortho carbon (atom 4) with f⁺ = 0.4964, indicating strong activation for nucleophilic aromatic attack. The most nucleophilic site is the meta carbon (atom 5) with f⁻ = 0.1073. The dual descriptor highlights the ortho/para positions (atoms 7–9) as the most activated for electrophilic aromatic substitution, consistent with the nitro group's electron-withdrawing directing effect. Charge conservation checks passed for both f⁺ and f⁻ (both sums equal 1.0). No warnings were reported.</prose>
<parameter name="value">0.49641781
